data.title: Runtime guards: command-v checks + missing base_url error + both-unavailable fallback
data.description:
As an implementation-plan agent running in a partially-deployed environment, I can rely on ref-library-loader.sh to degrade gracefully when dso ref-query or confluence-fetch.sh is absent.

Done definitions:
- Startup check for `command -v dso ref-query` that explicitly verifies subcommand registration (not just dso binary presence); absent → stderr warning, URL-only mode
- Startup check for `command -v confluence-fetch.sh`; absent → stderr warning, corpus-only mode
- Missing `confluence.base_url` in dso-config.conf → configuration error at startup, URL fetch path aborts, corpus-only mode with clear stderr error
- BOTH unavailable → stderr warning, tasks generated without citations, no error
- Story ba7d-729d's "both-unavailable" and guard-related sub-tests continue to pass after implementation
- Unit tests cover each degradation mode

Considerations:
- [Reliability] Subcommand-registration check is stricter than `command -v dso` — test both binary-absent and subcommand-absent paths
- [Maintainability] Centralize fallback logic in one function
- [Maintainability] Subcommand-registration check must suppress/separate its own stderr from the startup warning channel so unrelated dso init warnings are not confused with "ref-query unavailable"
